Harmful sugars produced by these microbes can spark immune responses that damage the brain. This breakthrough explains why some genetically at-risk people develop the diseases while others don’t.

articles.title: Your DNA has a secret “second code” that decides which genes get silenced

Scientists have discovered that cells can detect less efficient genetic instructions and selectively silence them. articles.title: The hidden tradeoff behind today’s most popular weight loss drugs

articles.title: Torpedo-like object discovered by Indonesian fisherman
articles.description: A fisherman in Indonesia discovered a torpedo-like object near the Lombok Strait, a waterway between Bali and Lombok.

articles.title: Melania Trump denies ties to Jeffrey Epstein and urges hearing for survivors
articles.description: She called rumours that Epstein introduced her to Donald Trump "mean-spirited attempts to defame my reputation".

articles.title: US has let in 4,499 refugees since October - all but three were South African
articles.description: Trump has given priority to Afrikaners, a white minority group he says is being persecuted. South Africa has objected to his characterisation.

articles.title: Vietnamese refugees will be left off Victims of Communism memorial: documents
articles.description: The memorial has faced criticism for cost overruns, delays and concerns that it could whitewash the history of alleged Nazi collaborators.

articles.title: Manor Park sidewalk plan will outlast current residents, councillors argue
articles.description: Some residents want them. Other don't. Councillors argued that making decision about what people want now could have long-term effects.
